mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative equity returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of triggered months across a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative median 60-day returns in 70% of cases over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects capacity utilization improvements, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with a Rank IC below 0.01 during recovery periods, or returns are flat/negative in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above 0.6% triggers immediate inflation fears and anticipated rate hikes, causing negative equity returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Negative returns fail to materialize in 60% of threshold crossings over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening that compresses corporate margins, yielding negative quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive median 60-day returns in 65% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in real GDP during a monetary tightening cycle confirm economic contraction, forcing equity repricing and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of contraction quarters during tightening regimes over a 4-year test.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production signals stable manufacturing output, supporting positive equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.015 across all rolling 2-year windows or positive returns occur in less than 50% of cases.
